per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA concerns the company passing data to third parties when legally required, while the REG text appears to concern disclosure information to the data subject/recipients; the constraints are not clearly the same or directly conflicting from the provided text.

The REA is about disclosing personal data to third parties under legal compulsion, whereas the REG is about making a report public and transmitting it to EU institutions; they address different acts and objects.

The REA states when data may be passed to third parties, while the REG requires the controller to provide information if disclosure is envisaged and specifies timing for that information; omission of the notice requirement in the REA is not itself a deviation.
